title: Cleome chiriquensis
intro: Cleome chiriquensis, also known as the Chiriqui bee plant, is a species of flowering plant in the Cleomaceae family, native to Central and South America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, making it a potential remedy for various health issues such as wounds, infections, and digestive problems. The bioactive constituents of Cleome chiriquensis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Cleome chiriquensis is a species of flowering plant in the Cleomaceae family. It belongs to the genus Cleome, which includes approximately 170 species of flowering plants. The Cleome genus is placed within the order Brassicales, a group of flowering plants that also includes mustard, cabbages, and broccoli. Cleome chiriquensis is native to Central America and is a member of the family Cleomaceae, which is a small family of flowering plants. The classification of Cleome chiriquensis reflects its close relationship to other plants within the Brassicales order.
